response.justification: Candidate has 13+ years of solid software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, with some developer-tooling exposure (CI/CD setup, MCP code generators, test frameworks like Trial and Fluent-Asserts). However, the resume shows no formal people management or team leadership experience, which is a hard requirement (2+ years). There is also no evidence of leading organizational change, no mobile-platform focus at the scale implied, and limited dedicated developer-tools/build-systems work compared to the 3-year requirement.
strengths: Strong full-stack engineering background with 13+ years across diverse stacks (Node.js, TypeScript, DLang, C/C++)
Demonstrated interest in developer tooling: built a test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), MCP code generators, and CI/CD infrastructure
Track record of knowledge sharing through workshops, talks, and courses (Selenium course, Web Internals, Refactoring Dojos)
concerns: No documented people management or direct-report leadership experience — fails the 2-year people management minimum
Lacks mobile-platform ecosystem experience at scale; iOS/Apple TV work was small-team product, not platform infrastructure
No evidence of strategic roadmap ownership, organizational change leadership, or managing engineering productivity at scale

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure work
